---
title: "Question 1 R code"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
date: "2025-03-07"
---

## Part 1(a) Sampling using Random Walk Metropolis-Hastings

```{r}
# Fix seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)
```

```{r}
# Define the target probability density function
laplace_pdf <- function(x) {
  return (0.5 * exp(-abs(x)))
}
```

```{r}
#Implementation Metropolis-Hastings random walk
mh_sampler <- function(N, s, x0 = 0) {
  output1 <- numeric(N)
  output1[1] <- x0
  
  for (i in 2:N) {
    proposal_x_star <- rnorm(1, mean = output1[i - 1], sd = s)
    acceptance_ratio1 <- laplace_pdf(proposal_x_star) / laplace_pdf(output1[i - 1])
    
    if (log(runif(1)) < log(acceptance_ratio1)) {
      output1[i] <- proposal_x_star
    } else {
      output1[i] <- output1[i - 1]
    }
  }
  
  return(output1)
}

```

```{r}
# Draw samples from the Laplace distribution using MH algorithm
N <- 10000
s <- 1
chain <- mh_sampler(N, s)
```

```{r}
# wrap result in a data frame for plotting
result_df <- data.frame(draws = chain)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)


# Sequence for true density curve
x_values <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 1000)
reference1 <- data.frame(x = x_values, y = laplace_pdf(x_values))

# Create plot with legend style
plot1 <- ggplot(result_df, aes(x = draws)) +
  
  # Histogram
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density.., fill = "Sample Histogram"), bins = 50, color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  
  # Kernel Density Estimate (KDE)
  geom_density(aes(color = "Kernel density estimate"), size = 1) +
  
  # True Laplace PDF
  geom_line(data = reference1, aes(x = x, y = y, linetype = "True Distribution f(x)"), color = "green", size = 1) +
  
  # Customize legend style
  scale_fill_manual(name = "", values = c("Sample Histogram" = "skyblue")) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "", values = c("Kernel density estimate" = "orange")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "", values = c("True Distribution f(x)" = "dashed")) +
  
  # Labels and title
  labs(title = "Metropolis-Hastings Sampling", x = "X", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal() +
  
  # Position legend at top
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Display the plot
print(plot1)

```

```{r}
# Estimate Monte Carlo mean and standard deviation
mean_est <- mean(chain)
sd_est <- sd(chain)
cat("Monte Carlo Mean:", mean_est, "\n")
cat("Monte Carlo SD:", sd_est, "\n")
```

## Part 1(b) Convergence Diagnostic (R-hat)

```{r}
# Define funtion to calculate R-hat statistic
rhat_statistic <- function(N, s, J, init_values) {
  all_chains <- matrix(NA, nrow = N, ncol = J)
  
  for (j in 1:J) {
    all_chains[, j] <- mh_sampler(N, s, init_values[j])
  }
  
  Mj <- colMeans(all_chains)
  Vj <- apply(all_chains, 2, var)
  
  W <- mean(Vj)
  M <- mean(Mj)
  B <- mean((Mj - M)^2)
  
  R_hat_value <- sqrt((B + W) / W)
  return(R_hat_value)
}
```

```{r}
# evaluate R-hat across different step sizes
s_values <- seq(0.001, 1, length.out = 50)
R_hat_values <- sapply(s_values, function(s) rhat_statistic(N = 2000, s = s, J = 4, init_values = c(-5, 0, 5, 10)))
```

```{r}
# organize results for plotting
rhat_data <- data.frame(s_values, R_hat_values)

# visualization of R-hat against step size
p <- ggplot(rhat_data, aes(x = s_values, y = R_hat_values)) +
  
  # R-hat curve
  geom_line(aes(color = "R-hat"), size = 1) +
  
  # Threshold line at 1.05
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 1.05, linetype = "Threshold (1.05)"), color = "red", size = 1) +
  
  # Customize legend
  scale_color_manual(name = "", values = c("R-hat" = "blue")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "", values = c("Threshold (1.05)" = "dashed")) +
  
  # Labels and theme
  labs(title = "R-hat vs Step Size", x = "Step size (s)", y = "R-hat") +
  theme_minimal() +
  
  # Position legend at top-right
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Display the plot
print(p)
```
